figures.caption: Figure 3: Recovering the Lorenz-63 dynamics on the support of finite snapshot data {ρj}mj=0 that covers the full chaotic attractor. In this example, MSE(vθ) = 1.48 · 10−2 and MSE(fθ) = 2.27 · 10−3.

Figure 4: Recovering the Lorenz-63 dynamics on the support of finite snapshot data {ρj}mj=0 which covers only a fraction of the chaotic attractor. The dynamics are accurately identified on the support of the observed density snapshots. In this example, MSE(vθ) = 8.48 · 10−2 and MSE(fθ) = 3.93 · 10−3.
Figure 2: Recovering a one-dimensional function from its pushforward action on five densities.
Figure 1: Flowchart of our main results.
